author: Dr. Lizard
title: What organs are most commonly affected in AL amyloidosis?
answer: The kidneys and heart are most commonly affected, occurring in 60 to 80 percent of patients.
detail: Kidney involvement occurs in 60 to 70 percent of patients, typically manifesting as nephrotic-range proteinuria and edema.
Cardiac involvement occurs in 70 to 80 percent of patients and is the leading cause of death.
Early cardiac signs include low voltage on electrocardiography, concentric ventricular thickening, and elevated cardiac biomarkers like troponin and NT-proBNP.
Nervous system involvement causes small-fiber neuropathy and autonomic dysfunction with orthostatic hypotension and gastrointestinal motility disturbances.
Macroglossia occurs in approximately 10 to 20 percent of patients with AL amyloidosis.
releaseNote: AL amyloidosis commonly affects the kidneys and heart, with kidney involvement occurring in 60-70% and cardiac involvement in 70-80% of patients. Early cardiac signs include low voltage on electrocardiography and elevated cardiac biomarkers.
